description: Generic MITRE ATT&CK technique detection for Python code, using flow analysis, API calls, imports, vulnerabilities, obfuscation, behavior features, and dangerous calls.

T1552.001.name: Credentials from Environment Variables
T1552.001.description: Adversaries may obtain credentials from environment variables.

T1552.003.name: Credentials in URLs
T1552.003.description: Adversaries may obtain credentials from URLs (e.g., in connection strings, basic auth).

T1056.001.name: Input Capture (Keylogging)
T1056.001.description: Adversaries may capture user input via keylogging.

T1528.name: Steal Application Access Token
T1528.description: Adversaries may steal application access tokens (e.g., OAuth, API keys).

exclusion_rules.name: Legitimate token usage in API calls

T1083.name: File and Directory Discovery
T1083.description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories.

T1046.name: Network Service Discovery
T1046.description: Adversaries may scan for network services.

T1059.006.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter (Python)
T1059.006.description: Adversaries may abuse Python to execute arbitrary code.

T1064.name: Dynamic Code Execution
T1064.description: Adversaries may use runtime code generation to execute arbitrary code.

T1129.name: Shared Modules (Dynamic Library Loading)
T1129.description: Adversaries may load shared libraries to execute arbitrary code.

T1547.001.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution (Registry)
T1547.001.description: Adversaries may modify registry run keys for persistence.

T1547.006.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution (Startup Folder)
T1547.006.description: Adversaries may place files in the startup folder for persistence.

T1053.003.name: Scheduled Task/Job (Cron)
T1053.003.description: Adversaries may use cron for persistence.

T1543.002.name: Systemd Service
T1543.002.description: Adversaries may create or modify systemd services for persistence.

T1546.004.name: Shell Configuration Modification (.bashrc, .profile)
T1546.004.description: Adversaries may modify shell configuration files for persistence.

T1505.name: Server Software Component (Backdoor)
T1505.description: Adversaries may install a backdoor into server software.

T1027.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
T1027.description: Adversaries may obfuscate files or information to evade detection.

T1140.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information
T1140.description: Adversaries may deobfuscate or decode files/information.

T1070.004.name: Indicator Removal (File Deletion)
T1070.004.description: Adversaries may delete files to hide their activity.

T1112.name: Modify Registry
T1112.description: Adversaries may modify the Windows Registry.

T1562.002.name: Disable Windows Event Logging
T1562.002.description: Adversaries may disable logging to hide their activity.

T1041.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
T1041.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data over a command and control channel.

T1567.002.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service (HTTP POST)
T1567.002.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data via HTTP POST.

T1048.002.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol (FTP)
T1048.002.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data over FTP.

T1048.003.name: Exfiltration Over Email (SMTP)
T1048.003.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data over SMTP.

T1486.name: Data Encrypted for Impact (Ransomware)
T1486.description: Adversaries may encrypt data to disrupt availability or demand ransom.

T1490.name: Inhibit System Recovery (Delete Backups)
T1490.description: Adversaries may delete or disable system recovery mechanisms.

T1485.name: Data Destruction
T1485.description: Adversaries may destroy data for impact.

T1105.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
T1105.description: Adversaries may download tools from C2 servers.

T1071.001.name: Application Layer Protocol (HTTP/HTTPS)
T1071.001.description: Adversaries may use HTTP/HTTPS for C2 communication.

T1071.004.name: Application Layer Protocol (DNS)
T1071.004.description: Adversaries may use DNS for C2.

T1132.001.name: Data Encoding (Base64)
T1132.001.description: Adversaries may encode data in Base64 for C2.

T1132.002.name: Data Encoding (Compression)
T1132.002.description: Adversaries may compress data for C2.

T1005.name: Data from Local System
T1005.description: Adversaries may collect data from local files.

T1074.001.name: Data Staged (Compression)
T1074.001.description: Adversaries may stage data by compressing it.

T1033.name: System Owner/User Discovery
T1033.description: Adversaries may discover the system owner or current user.

T1012.name: Query Registry
T1012.description: Adversaries may query the Windows Registry.

T1021.004.name: Remote Services (SSH)
T1021.004.description: Adversaries may use SSH for lateral movement.

T1021.002.name: Remote Services (SMB/Windows Admin)
T1021.002.description: Adversaries may use Windows admin shares for lateral movement.

T1548.003.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism (sudo)
T1548.003.description: Adversaries may abuse sudo to gain elevated privileges.

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to gain initial access or escalate.
